assertions.expectation: Explains that 'No channel' is a legacy configuration that lacks granular maintenance exclusion types
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'No channel' is legacy and explains it lacks granular exclusion types, showing in the comparison table that it only has 'basic 30-day no upgrades type' vs release channels having full scope options

assertions.expectation: Mentions that 'No channel' does not support 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion scope — only the 30-day 'no upgrades' type, AND per-nodepool maintenance exclusion is also limited
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'No channel' doesn't have 'No minor or node upgrades' exclusion (marked with ❌) and shows per-nodepool maintenance exclusion granularity is 'Limited to 30-day no upgrades only'

assertions.expectation: Notes that Extended support (24 months) is only available on release channels, not 'No channel'
assertions.reason: Comparison table clearly shows Extended support is available for release channels (✅) but 'Not available' for 'No channel' (❌), and mentions '24-month support available for 1.27+' under Extended channel benefits

assertions.expectation: Notes that rollout sequencing (an advanced feature) is not available on 'No channel'
assertions.reason: Comparison table shows rollout sequencing is available for release channels (✅) but 'Not available' for 'No channel' (❌), and includes fleet coordination as a feature

assertions.expectation: Recommends Regular or Stable channel as the closest migration target (legacy behavior matches Stable for minors, Regular for patches)
assertions.reason: Response recommends Regular or Extended channels, but does not mention Stable channel at all. The expectation specifically asks for Stable channel recommendation which is missing

assertions.expectation: Mentions Extended channel as a migration path for customers who want maximum flexibility around EoS enforcement
assertions.reason: Response includes a dedicated 'Extended Channel Alternative' section explaining Extended channel benefits including 'Minor version upgrades are NOT automated' and 'Better migration path from No channel mindset'

assertions.expectation: Warns about exclusion translation: when moving from 'No channel' to release channels, some exclusions don't translate 1:1 and may be ignored — only 'no_upgrades' type translates between the two. Recommends adding a temporary 'no upgrades' exclusion during the transition
assertions.reason: Response explicitly warns 'only exclusions of type no_upgrades translate between the configurations. Your current exclusions may be ignored after migration' and provides step-by-step migration with temporary protection using 'no_upgrades' exclusion

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to change the release channel
assertions.reason: Response provides the specific gcloud command: 'gcloud container clusters update CLUSTER_NAME --region REGION --release-channel regular' and similar commands for extended channel
Response does not reference or mention the specific URL cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/release-channels anywhere in the text
